label: PASS
rationale: After Set SPInfo.Enabled to false succeeds, the Locking SP is disabled immediately. A Locking table Get is not Authenticate, DeleteSP, or Set on SPInfo.Enabled, so it shall fail with SP_DISABLED.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target Get is an in-SP Locking operation after SPInfo.Enabled was set false. Disabled Locking SPs allow only the listed exceptions; this Get should fail with SP_DISABLED, not SUCCESS.

label: PASS
rationale: GetFreeRows on the Locking table is not one of the methods permitted while the SP is disabled. Since SPInfo.Enabled=false had completed, SP_DISABLED is the compliant result.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP remained disabled after SPInfo.Enabled=false, and GetFreeRows is not a re-enable/authentication/delete exception. A SUCCESS response is non-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The later Admin SP Frozen true then false only controls whether sessions may be opened. With Frozen false, session startup can succeed, but the Locking SP is still disabled, so the target Get shall fail with SP_DISABLED.

label: FAIL
rationale: Clearing Frozen permits opening a session, but it does not re-enable an SP disabled via SPInfo.Enabled=false. The target Locking table Get is not an allowed disabled-state method, so SUCCESS is non-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Although the SP was disabled, Set on the Enabled column of SPInfo is explicitly allowed to re-enable it. After the successful Set to true, the later GetFreeRows SUCCESS is protocol-compliant.
concerns: The evidence does not spell out GetFreeRows result contents, but the lifecycle state no longer requires SP_DISABLED.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful Set of SPInfo.Enabled to true is an allowed re-enable operation, so the SP is no longer disabled for the target GetFreeRows. Returning SP_DISABLED is therefore non-compliant.
concerns: The packet evidence supports lifecycle compliance for SUCCESS after re-enable; it does not independently validate the returned row count.
